name: Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon to the church of Saint-Simon - loop
name_original: Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon à l’église de Saint-Simon – boucle
region_intro: Hiking around Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon offers exploration of diverse natural landscapes. The region features the Gélise valley to the east and south, characterized by limestone cliffs. To the west and north, the landscape transitions into the dense Landes forest. A central plateau showcases a mosaic of meadows, vineyards, and cornfields, reflecting the area's agricultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon The most popular hiking route is Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon to the church of Saint-Simon - loop, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local terrain and passes by a historical church. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fourcès Medieval Village – Fourcès loop from Fourcès, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the medieval village and surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Source in Lesparre loop from Montréal, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon is defined by limestone cliffs, dense pine forests, and agricultural landscapes including vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The routes in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: Hiking in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon offers a diverse experience. You'll encounter the striking limestone cliffs of the Gélise valley to the east and south, and the dense pine trees of the Landes forest to the west and north. The central plateau features a mosaic of meadows, vineyards, and cornfields, reflecting the region's rich agricultural heritage.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting historical sites. You might encounter the 12th-century village church, the St-Pierre fountain, or the ancient fortified church of St-Simon. The region also has ancient cave dwellings carved into the soft rock, particularly in the Gélise valley. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000554910/saint-pe-saint-simon-to-the-church-of-saint-simon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon to the church of Saint-Simon - loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a historical church.

Q: What natural attractions can I find near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the diverse landscapes, you can explore the Gélise valley with its limestone cliffs and ancient cave dwellings. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6662940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Way of Marsan and de l'Armagnac&lt;/a&gt;, a former railroad line, offers a unique path through the countryside. The wider region also features natural scenery around Lac de l'Uby.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000554910/saint-pe-saint-simon-to-the-church-of-saint-simon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon to the church of Saint-Simon - loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.1-mile (9.8 km) trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon is enjoyable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, especially with the vineyards. Summer is also suitable, but it's advisable to hike in the mornings or late afternoons to avoid the midday heat. The varied landscapes, including forests and valleys, provide options for different weather conditions.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon suitable for families?
A: Yes, Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon offers trails suitable for various abilities, including easier options that can be great for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37614319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source in Lesparre loop from Montréal&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.9-mile (6.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 40 minutes, making it a good choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on most public hiking paths in rural France, provided they are kept under control, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and that you clean up after them.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: Given the rural nature of Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon and its focus on outdoor activities, parking is generally available near popular trailheads and village centers. It's common to find designated parking areas or roadside spots where you can leave your vehicle before starting your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: The hiking experience in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, which includes tranquil woods, picturesque vineyards, and the unique geological features of the Gélise valley. The well-preserved natural landscapes and varied terrain are frequently highlighted.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails available for exploration in Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon. These routes cater to various difficulty levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there any villages or towns I can visit along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, some hiking routes will lead you through or near charming local villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fourcès Medieval Village – Fourcès loop from Fourcès&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3679990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fourcès Medieval Village&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Montréal – Montréal (Gers) Village loop from Montréal&lt;/a&gt;, explores the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3908601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montréal (Gers) Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What types of agriculture will I see while hiking in the region?
A: The central plateau of Saint-Pé-Saint-Simon showcases a rich agricultural tapestry. You'll frequently walk past vineyards, which are significant to the local economy, producing renowned Ténarèze Armagnac and Floc de Gascogne. There are also extensive cornfields and meadows, maintained by mixed farming and small-scale farms, preserving a varied and picturesque landscape.
tips: The church of Saint-Simon dates from the 11th century or the 12th century. It is interesting to note that this church was built to defend and take refuge inside. The roof of the church was raised to build a defense room equipped with crossbows. At the end of the 15th or the beginning of the 16th century, a main building was added which was defended by loopholes.
